title: I AM A MIRACLE
text: Beginning a new year of recovery by accepting my powerlessness over alcohol. I find gratitude for being alive and sober today, a true miracle.

title: FIRST THINGS FIRST
text: Focusing on my primary purpose. Keeping sobriety first in my life. Through honesty and acceptance, I build a strong foundation for the day ahead.

title: THE DEGREE OF WILLINGNESS
text: Willingness is the key to letting go of my old ways. I must face my fears with honesty, admitting that my self-will was unmanageable.

title: ACCEPTING OUR HUMANITY
text: Acceptance is the answer. I am not perfect; I am a human being in recovery. I practice humility by accepting my character defects and asking for help.

title: DELIVERANCE FROM FEAR
text: Facing the evil, corroding thread of fear. I ask God to remove my fear and direct my attention to what He would have me be, finding faith and courage.

title: EXPECTATIONS VS. GRATITUDE
text: Resentment arises from unmet expectations. When I shift my focus from what I lack to what I have, resentment turns into gratitude and serenity.

title: THE CHALLENGE OF HONESTY
text: Rigorous honesty is the baseline. In my daily review, I check my motives. Am I being self-seeking, dishonest, or fearful? Honesty keeps me sober.

title: LEAP OF FAITH
text: On this extra day, I take a leap of faith. I turn my ego and self-will over, trusting that a Power greater than myself is guiding my steps.

title: THE SURRENDER OF EGO
text: Ego deflation is necessary for spiritual progress. I admit powerlessness and humility, stepping down from the pedestal of my own self-importance.

title: CLEANING HOUSE
text: We clean away the ruins of our past. By taking a fearless moral inventory, we uncover the resentments, anger, and fears that blocked us from God.

title: FREEDOM FROM RESENTMENT
text: Resentment is the number one offender. It destroys our spiritual life. Through prayer for those we resent, we find forgiveness, acceptance, and peace.

title: LOOKING INWARD
text: We begin a deep search of our character defects. In Step Four, we honestly look at our patterns of anger, jealousy, and self-pity.

title: OUT OF THE DARKNESS
text: Admitting our faults to another human being. In Step Five, we step out of the isolation of our secrets. Honesty brings relief and light.

title: A NEW FREEDOM
text: The promises of Step Nine begin to show. We know a new freedom and a new happiness. We do not regret the past, but find gratitude and acceptance.

title: ENTIRELY READY
text: Willingness to let go of our character defects. In Step Six, we ask ourselves if we are truly ready to have God remove our anger, pride, and ego.

title: TRUE HUMILITY
text: Humility is not weakness; it is a realistic acceptance of who I am. In Step Seven, I humbly ask God to remove my shortcomings, growing in grace.

title: RECONCILIATION
text: Making a list of all persons we have harmed. Step Eight prepares us for healing relationships. We face our past actions with honesty and willingness.

title: DIRECT AMENDS
text: Taking action to repair the past. In Step Nine, we make direct amends with courage and wisdom, restoring peace and overcoming lingering guilt.

title: THE ANSWER IS ACCEPTANCE
text: And acceptance is the answer to all my problems today. When I am disturbed, it is because I find some person, place, thing, or situation unacceptable.

title: DAILY MAINTENANCE
text: Sobriety is a daily reprieve. In Step Ten, we continue to take personal inventory. When we are wrong, we promptly admit it, avoiding anger and pride.

title: SHARING THE RECOVERY
text: Working with others brings joy. Practicing these principles in all our affairs. We carry the message, finding gratitude in seeing others recover.

title: CONSCIOUS CONTACT
text: Sought through prayer and meditation to improve our conscious contact with God as we understand Him. Seeking His will and the power to carry it out.

title: SENSITIVE TO OTHERS
text: Outgrowing the self-centeredness of alcoholism. I learn to listen, empathize, and practice kindness, replacing anger with love and understanding.

title: THE ROAD OF HAPPY DESTINY
text: Trudging the Road of Happy Destiny together. Sobriety is a shared journey of gratitude, acceptance, and joy, safe in the Fellowship of the Spirit.

title: A PROGRAM OF ACTION
text: AA is a program of action, not just theory. We take steps, make amends, work with others, and stay active to overcome self-pity and fear.

title: REDUCING PRIDE
text: Pride tells me I can handle everything myself. Humility shows me I need God and my fellows. Surrendering pride brings genuine peace and acceptance.

title: GIVING WITHOUT DEMAND
text: Giving freely of what we have found. Carrying the message without expecting any reward or credit. Selfless service is the ultimate antidote to ego.

title: THE SPIRITUAL AWAKENING
text: A spiritual awakening is a gradual change in our outlook and reactions. We find a new relationship with God, a new honesty, and freedom from fear.

title: BUILDING CHARACTER
text: Fulfilling our potential through spiritual growth. As we practice honesty, humility, and tolerance daily, our character defects slowly fade.

title: SOBRIETY IS A GIFT
text: Sobriety is not a personal achievement; it is a gift from a Power greater than myself. I maintain it through daily gratitude, service, and honesty.

title: OUR COMMON WELFARE
text: Group unity is essential. Our common welfare comes first. We practice love, tolerance, and service, setting aside personal ambitions for the group.

title: TRUSTED SERVANTS
text: Our leaders do not govern; they serve. We practice humility in service positions, trusting in the group conscience guided by God.

title: ATTRACTION, NOT PROMOTION
text: We carry the message by example, not by arguing. Our public relations policy is based on attraction. Let our changed lives draw others in.

title: THE BEDROCK OF UNITY
text: Anonymity is the spiritual foundation of all our traditions. It reminds us to place principles before personalities, practicing deep humility.

title: OUTSIDE ISSUES
text: AA has no opinion on outside issues. We avoid public controversies, preserving our energy and unity for our single primary purpose: carrying the message.

title: GRATITUDE IN ACTION
text: Gratitude is not just a feeling; it is an action. I express my gratitude by being of service, answering 12th-step calls, and helping newcomers.

title: A LIFE OF TRUST
text: Surrendering my fears of the future. I decide to trust God, taking one day at a time, practicing acceptance and staying open to His guidance.

title: THE SPIRIT OF SERVICE
text: Love and service are our primary goals. We help others without demanding credit, overcoming our ego and finding a deeper, spiritual joy.

title: AN INVENTORY OF JOY
text: Ending the year with a review of my blessings. I find deep gratitude for another year of sobriety, honesty, and fellowship. Serenity is mine.
